info.name: Approach2-SequentialFeedback-MultiContract-100K-30d
info.description:
APPROACH 2: SEQUENTIAL SIMULATION WITH FEEDBACK LOOP

PROBLEM SOLVED:
  ACTUS contract state is contract-scoped. TBill-A's MD event (+$24,500)
  does NOT update a shared cashBalance that the Liability contract can read.

  Approach 1 used PRE-COMPUTED static indexes (manual guesswork).
  Approach 2 DERIVES indexes from ACTUAL contract events.

HOW IT WORKS:
  Phase 1: Load exogenous data (PEG_RISK_SCORE — external to contracts)
  Phase 2: Run T-Bill contracts ONLY → capture actual IED/MD events
  Phase 3: Pre-request scripts DYNAMICALLY build SC_CASH, SC_TBILL_MV,
           SC_TOTAL_RESERVES indexes from Phase 2 actual events
  Phase 4: Register behavioral models + scenario with dynamic indexes
  Phase 5: Run Liability contract ONLY (Iteration 1) → capture PP events
  Phase 6: Rebuild indexes incorporating actual redemptions from Iter 1
  Phase 7: Re-run Liability (Iteration 2) with corrected indexes
  Phase 8: Convergence check — compare Iter 1 vs Iter 2

KEY IMPROVEMENT OVER APPROACH 1:
  SC_CASH index is built FROM actual T-bill maturity events, not guesswork.
  Maturity dates and payoff amounts come from the ACTUS engine, not us.

REMAINING LIMITATION:
  Redemptions in Iter 1 still use the no-redemption cash estimate.
  Iter 2 corrects this. Full convergence may need 3+ iterations.
  Approach 3 (Portfolio State Service) solves this with real-time middleware.

Open Postman Console (Ctrl+Alt+C) before running.

item.name: ═══ PHASE 1: Exogenous Data (Not Derived From Contracts) ═══

exec: console.log('APPROACH 2: SEQUENTIAL SIMULATION WITH FEEDBACK LOOP');
console.log('✅ Phase 1: PEG_RISK_SCORE loaded (EXOGENOUS — not derived from contracts)');
console.log('   This is the only index that is external market data.');
console.log('   All other indexes will be DERIVED from actual contract events.');

item.name: 1.2 Create minimal scenario for T-Bill run

exec: pm.test('Minimal scenario for T-Bill run', function(){
console.log('   T-Bills are deterministic (zero-coupon PAM) — no behavioral models needed.');
console.log('   Scenario required by API but T-Bills do not reference any risk factors.');

item.name: 2.1 Run T-Bill Simulation → Capture Actual Events

exec: console.log('PHASE 2: T-BILL ONLY SIMULATION — ACTUAL EVENTS');
// ── Collect T-bill contract metadata and events ──
// Known contract parameters (needed for MtM interpolation)
var payoff = (e.payoff !== undefined && e.payoff !== null) ? e.payoff : 0;
// ── Build daily timeline from actual events ──
for(var d = new Date(startDate); d <= endDate; d.setDate(d.getDate()+1)){
// For each T-bill, determine if alive on this date and compute MtM
// Check if actual MD event differs from expected
// T-bill is alive — compute interpolated MtM (pull to par)
// Maturity day — par value moves to cash
// else: matured in the past, no longer in portfolio
console.log('DERIVED DAILY TIMELINE (from actual T-Bill events):');
var matNote = dd.maturedToday ? ' ← ' + dd.maturedToday.cid + ' MD +$' + dd.maturedToday.payoff.toFixed(2) : '';
console.log('  ' + dd.date + ' | Cash=$' + dd.cash.toFixed(2) + ' | TBillMV=$' + dd.tbillMV.toFixed(2) + ' | Total=$' + dd.totalReserves.toFixed(2) + ' | Bills=' + dd.aliveBills + matNote);
console.log('✅ Daily data stored for Phase 3 dynamic index building.');
console.log('   Cash values are DERIVED from actual MD events — not guesswork.');
console.log('   ⚠️  Cash does NOT yet include redemptions (iteration 0).');

item.name: ═══ PHASE 3: Build DYNAMIC Indexes From T-Bill Events ═══

exec: // ── Build SC_TBILL_MV index dynamically from Phase 2 T-Bill events ──
console.log('✅ SC_TBILL_MV_DYN_I1: T-Bill MtM (DYNAMIC — from actual events)');
console.log('   Values computed by interpolating purchase price → par using');
console.log('   actual IED and MD dates from Phase 2 simulation.');

item.name: 3.2 Add SC_CASH_RESERVE (DYNAMIC Iter 1 — maturity inflows only)

exec: // ── Build SC_CASH index from actual T-Bill maturity events ──
// Iteration 1: Cash = initial + maturity inflows ONLY (no redemptions yet)
console.log('   Cash = $6,000 initial + actual T-Bill maturity inflows');
console.log('   ⚠️  Does NOT include redemption outflows (no PP data yet).');
console.log('   This is an OPTIMISTIC upper bound on cash.');
console.log('   Iteration 2 will subtract actual redemptions from Phase 5.');
console.log('   Reads SC_TOTAL_RESERVES (now DYNAMIC from T-Bill events)');
console.log('   Reads SC_CASH_RESERVE (now DYNAMIC, but no redemptions yet)');
console.log('✅ sc_seq_iter1_100k: Scenario with DYNAMIC indexes');
console.log('   3 dynamic indexes (derived from T-Bill events) + 1 exogenous + 2 models');
var payoff = (e.payoff !== undefined && e.payoff !== null) ? e.payoff : 0;
var nomVal = (e.nominalValue !== undefined && e.nominalValue !== null) ? e.nominalValue : null;
otherEvents.push({time: e.time, type: type, payoff: payoff, nominalValue: nomVal});
console.log('    a) BackingRatioModel did not fire (check monitoringEventTimes)');
console.log('    b) Model returned 0 for all dates (no stress detected)');
console.log('    c) stablecoinModels not recognized by engine');
var nomStr = pp.nominalValue !== null ? ' remaining=$' + pp.nominalValue.toFixed(2) : '';
console.log('  ' + pp.time + ' ' + pp.type + ' payoff=$' + pp.payoff.toFixed(2) + nomStr);
// ── Store PP events for Iteration 2 feedback ──
console.log('✅ Iteration 1 PP events stored for Phase 6 feedback loop.');
console.log('   Phase 6 will rebuild SC_CASH incorporating these actual redemptions.');

item.name: ═══ PHASE 6: FEEDBACK — Rebuild Indexes With Redemption Data ═══

exec: // FEEDBACK LOOP: Rebuild cash index using Iteration 1 redemptions
// Iter 1 cash = initial + maturity inflows (no redemptions)
// Iter 2 cash = initial + maturity inflows - ACTUAL redemptions from Iter 1
// Build a map of redemptions by date
// Rebuild cash timeline: start from iter0 (maturity inflows only)
// then subtract cumulative redemptions day by day
// Also store corrected daily data for reserves computation
console.log('Pre-request: Built Iter 2 cash index with ' + Object.keys(redemptionsByDate).length + ' redemption days');
console.log('✅ SC_CASH_DYN_I2: Cash with actual Iter 1 redemptions subtracted');
console.log('   This is MORE ACCURATE than Iter 1 because redemptions are real.');
console.log('✅ SC_RESERVES_DYN_I2: Total reserves corrected with redemptions');
console.log('✅ br_seq_100k_i2: BackingRatioModel (reads corrected Iter 2 indexes)');
console.log('✅ sc_seq_iter2_100k: Uses corrected Iter 2 indexes + same T-Bill MtM');
// ── Extract PP events for convergence comparison ──
var payoff = (e.payoff !== undefined && e.payoff !== null) ? e.payoff : 0;
var nomVal = (e.nominalValue !== undefined && e.nominalValue !== null) ? e.nominalValue : null;
iter2PP.push({time: e.time, type: e.type, payoff: payoff, nominalValue: nomVal});
console.log('  ⚠️  No PP/MRD events in Iter 2.');
var nomStr = pp.nominalValue !== null ? ' remaining=$' + pp.nominalValue.toFixed(2) : '';
console.log('  ' + pp.time + ' ' + pp.type + ' payoff=$' + pp.payoff.toFixed(2) + nomStr);
console.log('✅ Iteration 2 complete. Phase 8 will compare convergence.');
console.log('If Iter 1 and Iter 2 produce SIMILAR PP events, the feedback');
console.log('loop has converged — further iterations would not change results.');
console.log('If they DIFFER significantly, more iterations are needed,');
console.log('❌ Missing PP event data. Ensure Phases 5 and 7 ran.');
console.log('  Iteration 1 total redemptions: $' + totalI1.toFixed(2) + ' (' + iter1PP.length + ' PP events)');
console.log('  Iteration 2 total redemptions: $' + totalI2.toFixed(2) + ' (' + iter2PP.length + ' PP events)');
console.log('No PP events in either iteration to compare.');
console.log('This means the behavioral model did not trigger any redemptions.');
console.log('  a) The dynamic indexes showed healthy backing (no stress)');
console.log('  b) The BackingRatioModel is not being called by the engine');
console.log('   The feedback loop has stabilized.');
console.log('   Consider running Iteration 3, or use Approach 3 (middleware).');
// ── Rebuild full portfolio from T-Bill events + Iter 2 Liability events ──
// Build redemptions by date from Iter 2
// Compute portfolio state day by day
console.log('DATE         | CASH        | T-BILLS     | TOTAL RES   | SUPPLY      | BACKING  | LIQUIDITY | ACE  | SOURCE');
supply += redemptionsByDate[dd.date]; // negative payoff reduces supply
// Only print selected dates for readability
console.log('🚫 Stress dates: ' + stressDates[0] + ' to ' + stressDates[stressDates.length-1] + ' (' + stressDates.length + ' days)');
console.log('✅ No stress periods detected — all days passed ACE checks.');
console.log('  Cash:    DERIVED from actual T-Bill MD events (Phase 2)');
console.log('           + actual redemptions from Iter 2 PP events (Phase 7)');
console.log('  T-Bills: INTERPOLATED MtM from actual contract parameters');
console.log('  Supply:  $100K initial − cumulative Iter 2 redemptions');
console.log('  ✅ T-Bill MtM: derived from actual events, not pre-computed');
console.log('  ✅ Cash inflows: from actual MD events, not manual calculation');
console.log('  ✅ Redemptions: from actual Iter 2 PP events, not guesswork');
console.log('  → Approach 3 would update indexes IN REAL TIME during simulation');
